reasoning: The criteria specify that a successor released without a number will qualify if it expands on the original's capabilities and is recognized as a successor, similar to a new iPhone generation. In October 2025, Apple released an updated Apple Vision Pro featuring the M5 chip, replacing the original M2 version. Apple simultaneously discontinued the M2 model entirely. Tech press universally refers to this new M5 version as the "second-generation model," highlighting significant upgrades like processing power (M2 to M5), up to 120 Hz refresh rates, better battery life, and improved physical components like the Dual Knit Band. Because the M2 version was discontinued, and the M5 version seamlessly took its place as the definitive "second-generation" offering, it clearly meets the criteria for an unnumbered successor released before the December 31, 2025 deadline. The probability of YES is therefore extremely high.
